cot_content:
Okay, let's think about this question. The user is asking how to tell apart right and left earbuds without looking. The options are to tie a knot either at the connecting end or the speaker end of the left one.

First, I need to understand the parts of the earbuds. Earbuds typically have two main parts: the speaker part that goes into the ear and the connecting part that attaches to the wire or the device. If you're trying to distinguish left from right, the location of the knot would matter.

If you tie a knot at the connecting end of the left earbud (option A), then when you put them in your ears, the knot would be on the left side at the part that connects to the wire. But if you're using them, the connecting end is the part that's near the device or where the wire comes out, so maybe that's not as noticeable when you're wearing them. Also, if you're holding the earbuds in your hands, the connecting end is what you hold, so a knot there might be more obvious. But when you're wearing them, the knot would be at the back of your ear, maybe not as helpful.

Option B suggests tying a knot at the speaker end of the left earbud. The speaker end is the part that goes into the ear. If you tie a knot there, when you insert the earbud into your left ear, the knot would be on the outside. That might be more noticeable because you can feel or see it when you're wearing the earbuds. However, if the knot is at the speaker end, it might make it uncomfortable to wear or interfere with the fit.

But the question is about being able to tell them apart without looking. If the knot is at the connecting end (option A), when you're holding them, you can feel the knot at the part you're holding. That might help you remember which one is left. If the knot is at the speaker end (option B), when you're inserting them, you might feel the knot on the outside of the left earbud, helping you identify it.

I think the common advice is to tie a knot at the connecting end of the left earbud (option A) because that's the part you'll be holding when putting them in. When you're putting them in your ears, you can feel the knot at the end you're holding, which tells you it's the left one. This is a common tip for people who struggle to tell their left and right earbuds apart.
label: minority
